title: Display human build date in podman info
body:
Currently we are displaying the Seconds since EPOCH
this will change to displaying date, similar to `podman version`

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
More Quay issues, looks like. Restarted tests.
does this get exposed on the v2 API? if so, does it impact cockpit?

The version manpage has an example or two on it.  It would probably be good to verify and if necessary update those based on these changes.
LGTM other than prior comments.
Currently we are returning the EPOCH time to cockpit.  Which it can switch the display to human form.  The problem with Info is there is no good (easy) way to process this on the client side.
@TomSweeneyRedHat This is only for `podman info` not `podman version`.
@baude @mheon @TomSweeneyRedHat I took another pass at this and only added the BuildTime object to Version, so it should not effect any consumers.
Man page is just using current output of the podman info command.
@mheon I added another patch to fix a bug I found while improving the docs
LGTM. Will this PR and #5834 close #5736 ?

LGTM 
@mheon I'll let you press the final button on this one
